title: California Gap Z.8397 Memorial
crew_summary: 6 killed including pilot Sgt. Frank Hugh Neville Tothill
memorial_type: Memorial plaque on California Tavern building
narrative: On the night of 10/11 October 1941, Wellington II Z8397 of 12 Squadron took off from RAF Binbrook at 23:52 to attack Cologne. Shortly after departure, the aircraft developed engine problems and the pilot decided to return. Unable to reach Binbrook, Sgt Tothill attempted a crash-landing on the beach at California Gap near Caister-on-Sea, Norfolk, touching down at approximately 01:30. Some reports state that after coming to rest, the aircraft struck a mine on the beach which exploded, killing all six crew members. The memorial plaque on the California Tavern building commemorates these airmen.

crew.name: Frank Hugh Neville Tothill
crew.fate_detail: Killed when Wellington Z8397 crash-landed on the beach at California Gap and reportedly struck a mine. Buried at Moggerhanger (St. John) Churchyard.
crew.bio: Sgt Tothill was 25 years old, the son of Charity Wish Tothill of Moggerhanger, Bedfordshire.

crew.name: James Lennox Scott Dunlop
crew.burial_location: Stevenson (New Street) Cemetery, Section M, North Extension, Grave 22-23
crew.fate_detail: Killed in the crash. Canadian crew member buried at Stevenson Cemetery.

crew.name: Alfred Edward Cosgrove
crew.burial_location: Moston St. Joseph's Roman Catholic Cemetery, St. Anthony Section, Grave 622
crew.fate_detail: Known as 'Teddy'. Killed in the crash at California Gap. Buried at Moston Cemetery, Manchester.

crew.name: Aldwyn Anthony Nordon
crew.fate_detail: Killed in the crash. Buried at Barnsley (Carlton) Cemetery.

crew.name: Raymond Henry Todman
crew.fate_detail: Killed in the crash at California Gap.

crew.name: Arthur Pilkington
crew.fate_detail: Killed in the crash. Buried at Scottow Cemetery, Norfolk.

cause_of_loss: Engine problems after takeoff; crash-landed on beach; reportedly struck a mine after landing
memorial_details: Memorial plaque on the California Tavern building at California Gap, near Caister-on-Sea, Norfolk. IWM memorial reference 101410.

external_narratives.summary: Aircrew Remembered documents the loss of Wellington Z8397 of 12 Squadron on 10/11 October 1941. The aircraft took off from RAF Binbrook to attack Cologne but returned with engine problems and crash-landed on the beach at California Gap near Caister-on-Sea. Reports suggest the aircraft struck a mine after landing, killing all six crew.
key_details: Crashed on beach at California Gap
Reports of aircraft touching a mine after landing

external_narratives.summary: IWM records the memorial to the crew of Wellington Z8397 at California Gap, Norfolk. The memorial plaque is located on the California Tavern building.
key_details: Memorial plaque on California Tavern building

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep
